verses.text: These are the words Moses spake unto all Israel on this side Jordan in the wilderness. Eleven days from Horeb to Kadesh-barnea — yet it took forty years. In the fortieth year, eleventh month, first day, after the kings Sihon and Og were slain, Moses began to declare this law in the land of Moab. Guidance: Eleven days versus forty years — the distance between geography and obedience. The whole book of Deuteronomy is Moses telling the new generation what happened, and why, so they won't repeat it. History remembered is the cheapest tuition.

The LORD spake to us in Horeb: ye have dwelt long enough in this mount; turn, take your journey to the mount of the Amorites. I have set the land before you: go in, possess the land which the LORD sware unto Abraham, Isaac, Jacob. Guidance: 'Long enough.' Sinai was glorious — but it was a starting line, not a destination. God's love is restless on our behalf; He will not let us pitch a tent at the place He intended as a doorway. Set, sworn, given — only the going is left.

I said: I am not able alone to bear you. The LORD hath multiplied you as the stars of heaven. Take wise, understanding, known men of your tribes; I will make them rulers. I made them captains over thousands, hundreds, fifties, tens. I charged the judges: judge righteously between every man and the stranger. Respect not persons; hear small and great alike; for judgment is God's. Guidance: A leader who admits limits builds a system. Moses can't carry millions — so he distributes authority. The judges' charter is striking: no bias, no fear, no friendship. Justice is God's, not theirs to flavor.

We departed Horeb through the great terrible wilderness to Kadesh-barnea. I said: ye are come unto the mountain of the Amorites which the LORD doth give us. Go up, possess it. Fear not. Ye said: we will send men to search the land. I took twelve, one of every tribe. They brought back fruit, and said: it is a good land. Guidance: The plan was already given — go up. The spies were the people's idea, not God's. Even so, He let them go, and even the report was good. Sometimes God grants a detour to expose what's already in the heart.

Ye would not go up, rebelled against the LORD. Ye murmured: the LORD hated us, brought us out to destroy us. I said: dread not. The LORD goeth before you, He shall fight for you, as He did in Egypt — and in the wilderness, where the LORD thy God bare thee as a man doth bear his son. Yet in this ye did not believe. Guidance: A staggering image — the LORD carrying His people like a father carries a tired child. Forty years of being held, and they still doubted Him. Unbelief is not lack of evidence; it is the refusal to read what is right in your arms.

The LORD sware: not one of this evil generation shall see the good land, save Caleb — he wholly followed the LORD. The LORD was angry with me for your sakes: thou shalt not go in. But Joshua shall go; encourage him. Your little ones, whom ye said should be a prey, they shall go in. As for you, turn into the wilderness by the Red sea. Guidance: God's mercy and severity in one sentence. The doomed are real, the saved are real, the next leader is named. Moses is barred too — for your sakes — a hard line spoken without bitterness. Even the prophet pays for the people's sin.

Then ye answered: we have sinned, we will go up and fight. The LORD said: go not up, I am not among you, lest ye be smitten. Ye would not hear, went presumptuously up. The Amorites chased you as bees, destroyed you in Seir, even unto Hormah. Ye returned and wept before the LORD, but He would not hearken. Ye abode in Kadesh many days. Guidance: 'We have sinned' said too late. They wouldn't go when God said go; they went when He said stay. The bee swarm at Hormah teaches what no sermon could — God's no is not a suggestion, and presumption is rebellion in optimism's clothes.
